title: Filter aggregated results using HAVING clause
description: Filter aggregated results using HAVING clause. Key topics: HAVING filters after GROUP BY — WHERE filters before GROUP BY. HAVING can reference aggregated fields (SUM, COUNT, etc.). WHERE cannot reference aggregated fields — use HAVING instead.

sections.title: Key Learning Points
sections.description:
◆HAVING filters after GROUP BY — WHERE filters before GROUP BY
◆HAVING can reference aggregated fields (SUM, COUNT, etc.)
◆WHERE cannot reference aggregated fields — use HAVING instead
◆HAVING conditions can include AND/OR just like WHERE

sections.title: CDS Source Code

content:
Key Learning Points

◆HAVING filters after GROUP BY — WHERE filters before GROUP BY
◆HAVING can reference aggregated fields (SUM, COUNT, etc.)
◆WHERE cannot reference aggregated fields — use HAVING instead
◆HAVING conditions can include AND/OR just like WHERE

CDS Source Code

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZPRAC_SQL_HAVING'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'Practice CDS - HAVING clause on aggregated results'
define view ZPRAC_CDS_HAVING
  as select from ZPRAC_CDS_GROUP_BY
{
  key CustomerID,
      BookingCount,
      DistinctAirlines,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      TotalSpent,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      AvgFlightPrice,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      MinFlightPrice,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      MaxFlightPrice,
      CurrencyCode
}
having
      TotalSpent > 1000
  and BookingCount >= 3
  and DistinctAirlines > 1
details:
EXECUTION ORDER (conceptual):

FROM → WHERE → GROUP BY → HAVING → SELECT → ORDER BY

USE CASE:

Find customers who are high-value: total spend > 1000 AND

at least 3 bookings. Only interested in customers with

more than 1 distinct airline (they fly different carriers).
topic: HAVING — filtering AFTER GROUP BY aggregation
